meaning: n.(尤指在战场上的)残杀,大屠杀,流血
examples: Take it as you will: a smorgasbord of vehicular carnage or a condensed physics lesson.
Most of the carnage came two hours later, in a different building filled with classrooms.
When was the last time you heard an anti-nuclear campaigner drawing attention to this daily carnage?
There's evidence that the carnage has begun, albeit mostly in a few overheated condominium markets.
The carnage we see to talented kids at this age who reach the spotlight is dismaying.
